goal: Become a Data Scientist / ML Engineer
description: A comprehensive path from Python basics to deploying production ML models and working with LLMs.

steps.title: Learn Python Programming
steps.why: Python is the primary language for data science, ML, and AI. Every tool in this track is Python-based.
steps.milestone: Build a small automation script or data parser

steps.title: Master Mathematics & Statistics
steps.why: ML algorithms are built on math. Understanding the theory helps you choose the right model and debug issues.
steps.milestone: Solve probability problems and implement basic stats from scratch

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: Essential for tracking experiments, collaborating, and showcasing your projects to employers.
steps.milestone: Push your first project to GitHub

steps.title: Data Manipulation with Pandas & NumPy
steps.why: Real-world data is messy. You need to clean, transform, and explore data before any modeling.
steps.milestone: Complete an EDA (Exploratory Data Analysis) on a Kaggle dataset

steps.title: Data Visualization
steps.why: Communicating insights visually is as important as finding them. Stakeholders need clear charts, not raw numbers.
steps.milestone: Build a dashboard with 5+ different chart types

steps.title: Databases: SQL & NoSQL
steps.why: Data scientists frequently query databases. SQL is a non-negotiable skill in any data role.
steps.milestone: Write complex SQL queries with JOINs, CTEs, and window functions

steps.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
steps.why: The core of the track. Learn how models learn from data and how to evaluate them properly.
steps.milestone: Build and evaluate 3 different ML models on a real dataset

steps.title: Deep Learning & Neural Networks
steps.why: Powers computer vision, NLP, and generative AI. Required for any advanced AI role.
steps.milestone: Train an image classifier and an LSTM for sequence prediction

steps.title: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
steps.why: NLP and LLMs are the hottest area in AI right now. RAG, chatbots, and AI assistants all rely on this.
steps.milestone: Build a semantic search engine or fine-tune a BERT model

steps.title: MLOps & Model Deployment
steps.why: A model in a notebook is worthless. Learn to ship models to production and monitor them.
steps.milestone: Deploy an ML model as a REST API using FastAPI + Docker

description: Go from zero to building professional, responsive, and performant web applications using modern tools.

steps.title: Learn HTML & CSS
steps.why: The building blocks of the web. Every website is HTML for structure and CSS for style.
steps.milestone: Clone a real website layout using only HTML & CSS

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: You'll use Git every single day as a developer. Start early to build the habit.
steps.milestone: Host your HTML project on GitHub Pages

steps.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
steps.why: JS makes websites interactive. It's the only programming language that runs natively in browsers.
steps.milestone: Build a to-do app with full CRUD operations

steps.title: TypeScript
steps.why: TypeScript catches bugs before runtime. Almost all modern React projects use TypeScript.
steps.milestone: Convert a JavaScript project to TypeScript

steps.title: React.js
steps.why: The most in-demand frontend framework. Used by Facebook, Airbnb, Netflix, and thousands of startups.
steps.milestone: Build a full CRUD app with React Router and state management

steps.title: Frontend Performance & Testing
steps.why: Employers expect optimized, tested code. Performance and testing skills separate junior from senior devs.
steps.milestone: Achieve 90+ Lighthouse score and write unit + E2E tests

description: Build robust, scalable server-side applications and APIs that power modern web and mobile apps.

steps.title: Learn Python Programming
steps.why: Python is widely used for backend development and has excellent libraries for building APIs and services.
steps.milestone: Build CLI tools and basic scripts in Python

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: Version control is mandatory in any professional development environment.
steps.milestone: Collaborate on a repo using branches and pull requests

steps.title: Linux & Command Line
steps.why: Backend servers run on Linux. You need to be comfortable in a terminal environment.
steps.milestone: Write shell scripts to automate file management tasks

steps.title: Databases: SQL & NoSQL
steps.why: Every backend application stores data. Knowing how to design and query databases is fundamental.
steps.milestone: Design a relational schema and build queries with JOINs and transactions

steps.title: Backend Development with FastAPI
steps.why: FastAPI is modern, fast, and production-ready. It auto-generates API docs and enforces validation.
steps.milestone: Build a fully documented REST API with CRUD and authentication

steps.title: Authentication & Security Basics
steps.why: Insecure APIs are a major liability. Every backend developer must understand auth and security.
steps.milestone: Add JWT-based auth and role-based access control to your API

steps.title: Docker & Containerization
steps.why: Docker ensures your app runs the same everywhere. It's the standard for shipping backend services.
steps.milestone: Dockerize your FastAPI app and run it with Docker Compose

steps.title: System Design
steps.why: Senior backend roles require designing for scale. Understanding caching, queues, and microservices is key.
steps.milestone: Design and present architecture for a scalable e-commerce backend

description: Automate software delivery, manage cloud infrastructure, and ensure reliable, scalable systems.

steps.title: Linux & Command Line
steps.why: All cloud servers run Linux. This is the most fundamental skill for any DevOps engineer.
steps.milestone: Administer a Linux server: users, permissions, networking, cron jobs

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: DevOps is about automating developer workflows. Git is at the center of every pipeline.
steps.milestone: Manage a repo with branching strategy and protected main branch

steps.title: Networking Fundamentals
steps.why: Understanding how networks work is critical for configuring cloud VPCs, load balancers, and firewalls.
steps.milestone: Analyze network traffic with Wireshark and configure basic firewall rules

steps.title: Docker & Containerization
steps.why: Containers are the foundation of modern DevOps. Everything runs in containers today.
steps.milestone: Containerize a multi-service app with Docker Compose

steps.title: CI/CD Pipelines
steps.why: Automating build, test, and deploy cycles is the core value proposition of DevOps.
steps.milestone: Build a full CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions that tests and deploys to a server

steps.title: Cloud Platforms (AWS / GCP / Azure)
steps.why: Cloud is where all modern infrastructure lives. AWS is the market leader with the most job demand.
steps.milestone: Deploy a containerized app on AWS with EC2, RDS, and S3. Get AWS CCP certified.

steps.title: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
steps.why: Manually clicking in cloud consoles is error-prone. IaC lets you version and automate your infrastructure.
steps.milestone: Provision a full AWS environment (VPC, EC2, RDS) with Terraform

description: Build and ship professional Android applications using Kotlin and modern Jetpack libraries.

steps.title: Learn Kotlin for Android
steps.why: Kotlin is Google's official Android language. Modern Android development is 100% Kotlin.
steps.milestone: Write Kotlin programs using coroutines, data classes, and extension functions

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: Essential for tracking your app versions and collaborating with teams.
steps.milestone: Set up a GitHub repo for your Android project

steps.title: Android UI with Jetpack Compose
steps.why: Compose is the modern, declarative way to build Android UIs. Google recommends it for all new projects.
steps.milestone: Build a multi-screen app with Compose navigation and animations

steps.title: Android Architecture (MVVM)
steps.why: Professional apps use clean architecture. MVVM with ViewModel and Room is the industry standard.
steps.milestone: Build a notes app with Room database and ViewModel following MVVM

steps.title: Networking & APIs in Android
steps.why: Real apps consume APIs. Retrofit with Coroutines is the standard for Android networking.
steps.milestone: Build an app that fetches data from a public API and displays it offline-first

steps.title: Publishing to Google Play
steps.why: Shipping is a skill. Understanding the release process is required to be a professional developer.
steps.milestone: Publish your first app to the Google Play Store (even a simple one)

description: Learn to think like an attacker and defend systems. Go from networking basics to ethical hacking and SOC operations.

steps.title: Linux & Command Line
steps.why: Kali Linux and most security tools run on Linux. Comfort with the terminal is non-negotiable.
steps.milestone: Use Linux commands confidently for file ops, networking, and process management

steps.title: Networking Fundamentals
steps.why: You can't attack or defend what you don't understand. Networking is the foundation of cybersecurity.
steps.milestone: Set up a home lab, capture and analyze packets with Wireshark

steps.title: Python for Security Scripting
steps.why: Security professionals script their own tools: scanners, exploits, and automation scripts.
steps.milestone: Write a port scanner and a basic network sniffer in Python

steps.title: Learn Git & GitHub
steps.why: Storing and sharing your security tools, CTF writeups, and scripts professionally.
steps.milestone: Publish your security scripts to a GitHub portfolio

steps.title: Cryptography & PKI
steps.why: Encryption is everywhere in security. Understanding TLS, hashing, and certificates is essential.
steps.milestone: Implement common cryptographic algorithms and break weak ones in CTF challenges

steps.title: Ethical Hacking & Penetration Testing
steps.why: Understanding attack techniques is required to build effective defenses. Think like the adversary.
steps.milestone: Complete 10 HackTheBox machines and document your methodology

steps.title: Web Application Security
steps.why: Most modern attacks target web apps. OWASP Top 10 knowledge is required for any security role.
steps.milestone: Complete OWASP WebGoat and find vulnerabilities in a test app with Burp Suite

steps.title: Security Operations & Incident Response
steps.why: SOC and IR skills are in massive demand. Blue team skills complement offensive knowledge.
steps.milestone: Set up a SIEM, create detection rules, and simulate an incident response
